# GitHub repository metadata

Use these values when setting up or updating the GitHub repository.

## Short description (≤120 chars — shown on repo page)

```
The Claude Code starter kit. One command installs curated skills, agents, MCPs, hooks, and safety rails.
```

## Alternate descriptions (pick one)

- `One-command Claude Code starter kit: curated skills, agents, MCPs, and safety hooks. Framework-aware.`
- `npm install layer for Claude Code — skills, agents, commands, hooks, and local-first MCPs, pre-wired.`
- `The opinionated Claude Code starter kit. Run one command, get a production-ready AI coding environment.`

## Website

```
https://www.npmjs.com/package/clanker-code
```

## Topics / tags (for discoverability)

Copy-paste into GitHub's "Add topics" field:

```
claude-code
claude
anthropic
ai-coding
cli
npm-package
starter-kit
mcp
model-context-protocol
developer-tools
ai-tools
agents
skills
hooks
nodejs
typescript
```

## Setting up the repo

1. Create the repository at `github.com/umayer/clanker-code`.
2. Paste the short description into the "Description" field.
3. Set the website to the npm URL (or leave empty until published).
4. Click "Add topics" and paste the topics list.
5. Enable Issues, Discussions (optional).
6. Add a social-preview image (optional — 1280×640 with the logo + tagline).
7. Set the default branch to `main`.
8. In "Features," keep Issues on; Wiki off (use `docs/` or README instead).

## Suggested branch protection for `main` (once there are collaborators)

- Require pull request before merging
- Require status checks (test workflow)
- Require linear history
- Dismiss stale PR approvals when new commits are pushed

## After first npm publish

Update the website URL to `https://www.npmjs.com/package/clanker-code` and add a badge row to the top of the README.
